name: Almodóvar del Campo and Valle de Alcudia Loop
name_original: Almodóvar del Campo — ruta circular por el Valle de Alcudia
description: This difficult 55.5-mile touring cycling route through Valle de Alcudia offers diverse landscapes and historical sites near Almodóvar del Ca
region_intro: Touring cycling around Almodóvar Del Campo offers routes through a diverse landscape in the province of Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha. The region is characterized by its unique volcanic origins, with the town built on an ancient volcano and the landscape featuring lagoons and craters. A significant portion of the area lies within the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, known for its Mediterranean forest and varied topography. Cyclists can explore a mosaic of cultivated fields, hills, and geological formations. Best touring cycling routes around Almodóvar Del Campo The most popular touring cycling route is Climb to the Castle Convent of Calatrava La Nueva — circular from Puertollano, a 44.9 miles (72.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Villamayor de Calatrava from Puertollano — circular via Almodóvar del Campo, a moderate 38.8 miles (62.4 km) path. This route passes through the town of Almodóvar del Campo, offering views of the surrounding countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Almodóvar del Campo and Valle de Alcudia Loop, a 55.5 miles (89.3 km) trail leading through the Valle de Alcudia, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Almodóvar Del Campo is defined by volcanic terrain, the extensive Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, and cultivated plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Almodóvar Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almodóvar Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes around Almodóvar Del Campo, offering a wide variety of options for exploring the region's diverse landscapes. These routes range in difficulty from easy to challenging, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: The terrain around Almodóvar Del Campo is quite varied, characterized by its unique volcanic origins, extensive natural parks, and historical elements. You'll encounter a mosaic of cultivated fields, hills, and geological formations, including features like ancient volcanoes and lagoons. Routes often traverse the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375120615/almodovar-del-campo-and-valle-de-alcudia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Alcudia&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate or difficult, there are approximately 7 easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes allow you to enjoy the scenery without significant elevation challenges.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling?
A: Almodóvar Del Campo's routes offer access to several natural and historical landmarks. You can explore areas with volcanic origins, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, or visit natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7199638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Agria&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features beautiful lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7286202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Blanca&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7126612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Carrizosa&lt;/a&gt;, and even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6470149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de Ventillas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Almodóvar Del Campo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052875068/climb-to-the-castle-convent-of-calatrava-la-nueva-circular-from-puertollano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Castle Convent of Calatrava La Nueva — circular from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a substantial ride with significant elevation gain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almodóvar Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views, and the opportunity to explore both natural parks and historical sites.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through towns or villages?
A: Absolutely. Some routes are designed to pass through local towns and villages, offering opportunities to experience the local culture and find amenities. For instance, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052674274/villamayor-de-calatrava-from-puertollano-circular-via-almodovar-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamayor de Calatrava from Puertollano — circular via Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt; takes you through Almodóvar del Campo itself, providing views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Almodóvar Del Campo offers a good selection of challenging routes. Approximately 35 routes are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375120615/almodovar-del-campo-and-valle-de-alcudia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar del Campo and Valle de Alcudia Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult 89.3 km trail with substantial climbing.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for cycling in Castilla-La Mancha, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather than the summer heat or winter cold. These seasons are ideal for enjoying the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can find routes that lead to or pass by historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de la Villa&lt;/a&gt;. The broader area also includes the archaeological site of Sisapo, a former Roman city, offering a glimpse into the past.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona?
A: The Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, which covers a significant portion of the area, is renowned for its outstanding ecological value. It's a sanctuary for emblematic and endangered species, boasting the most complete representation of the Mediterranean forest on the Iberian Peninsula. While cycling, you might observe various bird species and other wildlife native to this rich ecosystem.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the diverse topography of Almodóvar Del Campo includes several viewpoints. The Region Research mentions the Mirador De La Ermita de Santa Brígida and the Cerro de los Molinos, which offer extensive panoramic views over the Sierra de Almodóvar, the Tirteafuera Depression, and the Almodóvar-Argamasilla Depression, showcasing the volcanic landscape and vast plains.
tips: In the Alcudia Valley, we find the municipality of Almodóvar del Campo built on the side of a volcano, so present in Campo de Calatrava, and whose crater is now a lagoon. The Arab footprint is the most emblematic of the city, leaving behind the memory of the Castle and above all the unique Parish of Our Lady of the Assumption. There you can see the first videomapping in Spain permanently installed in a closed space, under the theme of the life and work of San Juan de Ávila.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Valle de Alcudia region experiences hot summers, so the most comfortable times to cycle are during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are often at their most vibrant, making for a more enjoyable touring bicycle experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Almodóvar del Campo — circular route through the Valle de Alcudia?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road ride, featuring mostly well-paved surfaces suitable for touring bicycles. However, be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved, which could add a bit more challenge. The route includes an elevation gain of approximately 866 meters, so a good level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or fees for cycling in the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling on established routes within the Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to stay on marked paths, respect wildlife, and follow general park rules regarding waste disposal and noise. If you plan any off-route exploration, check with local authorities.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Almodóvar del Campo cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the town of Almodóvar del Campo, which serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. Look for public parking areas within the town, especially near the historic center, to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification, approximately 89 km distance, and over 800 meters of elevation gain, this route is generally not recommended for beginner touring cyclists or young families. It requires a good level of fitness and experience with longer distances and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You'll traverse the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park, with views of the Sierra de Almodóvar and unique volcanic formations. Historically, the area is known for its connection to Cervantes' works, with remnants of the old Royal Road and sites like the Venta de la Inés. Almodóvar del Campo itself is built on a volcano, with its crater now a lagoon.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this touring bicycle route briefly intersects with sections of other official trails. You'll encounter small portions of the Cañada Real Segoviana, Los Pozuelos de Calatrava - La Viñuela, and Viñuela - Veredas - Puente de Alcudia.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas, this is a long touring bicycle route that includes both paved and unpaved sections, and passes through a natural park. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is well-conditioned for such a distance and can keep pace safely, or consider alternative arrangements. Always keep dogs under control and respect local wildlife regulations.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park, it's an excellent area for ornithology. You may have opportunities to spot various bird species. The park is also home to endangered animal species, so keep an eye out, but always observe from a respectful distance to avoid disturbing them.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: The town of Almodóvar del Campo, being the start and end point, offers amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. As this is a circular route, planning your main stops around the town is advisable, as services may be limited in more remote sections of the Valle de Alcudia.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this challenging touring bicycle route, essential items include sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and a first-aid kit. Given the distance and elevation, consider bringing extra layers and checking the weather forecast before you depart.
